parsed.summary: Cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff carries high integration risk: a core module (process.mjs) was deleted outright, the primary API client (glm-client.mjs) was heavily modified without a dedicated test file, and a new retry+error-dispatch subsystem was bolted on without visible end-to-end integration tests. Any one of these is defensible in isolation; combined in a single cumulative sweep they create compounding failure surface. Recommend blocking until client-level and cross-module integration gaps are closed.

findings.title: Deleted scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) — orphaned import risk unverified
findings.body: The diff stat shows scripts/lib/process.mjs was completely removed (67 lines, 0 added). scripts/check-imports.mjs was modified by 5 lines in the same sweep, suggesting awareness, but static import checkers routinely miss dynamic import() with template literals, conditional requires, or references in untested branches. A runtime MODULE_NOT_FOUND on any surviving import path is a silent, unrecoverable crash that won't surface until the specific code path executes in production. No test file validates that no code path references the deleted module.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs\|from.*process' scripts/ commands/` to confirm zero surviving references. Add a CI gate that does a dry-run module resolution (e.g., `node --check` or dynamic import probe) across every entry point to catch stale imports before merge.

findings.title: scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs heavily modified with no dedicated unit test file
findings.body: glm-client.mjs shows 155 lines of net changes — the largest delta in any lib module — yet no tests/glm-client.test.mjs file appears in the diff. Every other lib module of comparable importance (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, fs.mjs, state.mjs, args.mjs, preset-config.mjs, render.mjs) received its own dedicated test file in the same sweep. The client is the single critical path for all API communication; a regression here directly affects every user-facing operation. tests/result-propagation.test.mjs (69 lines) may exercise some client paths but is too small to cover 155 lines of client changes.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs covering: request construction, error mapping via bigmodel-errors.mjs, retry integration via retry.mjs, timeout handling, and response validation. At minimum, mock-based unit tests for every exported function before shipping.

findings.title: New retry + BigModel error dispatch + client chain lacks integration test coverage
findings.body: Three new/modified modules form a tight error-handling chain: retry.mjs (203 lines, new), bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines, new), and glm-client.mjs (155 lines, modified). Each has unit tests in isolation (retry.test.mjs at 352 lines, bigmodel-errors.test.mjs at 189 lines), but no test file exercises the end-to-end path: client call → BigModel error → error dispatch → retry decision → backoff → re-invocation. This is exactly the kind of cross-module interaction where integration bugs hide — especially around retryable vs. non-retryable error classification and state consistency across retries.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration test that simulates a flaky BigModel API: first N calls return retryable errors, call N+1 succeeds. Verify correct retry count, backoff timing, error classification propagation, and that no state is corrupted across the retry boundary. This should cover the full glm-client → bigmodel-errors → retry round-trip.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs and job-control.mjs modified without dedicated test coverage
findings.body: tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) and job-control.mjs (20 lines changed) handle job lifecycle state — a domain where partial failures and race conditions are most dangerous. Neither has a dedicated test file in the diff. tests/job-render.test.mjs (144 lines) may exercise render paths but job-control state transitions under concurrent or failure scenarios appear untested. Job tracking state corruption could lead to lost jobs, stuck queues, or duplicate execution.
findings.recommendation: Add tests for tracked-jobs and job-control covering: job creation, state transitions (pending → running → completed/failed), concurrent job limits, and crash-recovery (can the system recover consistent state after a mid-transition failure).

next_steps: Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs\|from.*["'\'''].*/process["'\''']' scripts/ commands/` from repo root and confirm zero results — attach output as evidence.
Create tests/glm-client.test.mjs with mocked transport covering request/response/error/retry paths; require passing before v0.4.7.
Add one integration test exercising the full glm-client → bigmodel-errors → retry chain with simulated transient failures.
Verify tests/job-render.test.mjs actually exercises tracked-jobs.mjs and job-control.mjs state transitions; if not, add dedicated tests.
Run the full test suite (`npm test` or equivalent) and confirm zero failures on a clean checkout before tagging v0.4.7.
